0.b1: He wanted to make things better.
0.ai_notes: 'improvements': changes or additions that make something better than it was before.
0.remarks: 'improvements': 使某事物比以前更好的改变或增加。

1.original: All day, this third salesman gave me his ideas for getting new business,
1.b1: All day, this third salesman shared his ideas for getting new customers.
1.cn: 这一整天，第三位推销员都在跟我分享他获取新业务的想法，
1.ai_notes: 'getting new business': a common phrase in sales meaning to find new customers or earn new contracts.
1.remarks: 'getting new business': 销售中的常用语，指寻找新客户或获得新合同。

2.original: providing better service to customers, reducing wasted time, revising the compensation plan to give more incentive,
2.b1: He suggested better customer service, saving time, and changing the pay plan to motivate staff.
2.cn: 包括提供更好的客户服务、减少时间浪费、修改薪酬计划以提供更多激励，
2.ai_notes:
'compensation plan': the total package of salary, bonuses, and benefits offered to an employee.
'incentive': something that encourages a person to work harder or do something.
2.remarks:
'compensation plan': 提供给员工的薪水、奖金和福利的总方案。
'incentive': 鼓励某人更努力工作或做某事的事物。

3.original: all so that he and the company would make more.
3.b1: He did this so he and the company could earn more money.
3.cn: 这一切都是为了让他自己和公司能赚到更多钱。
3.ai_notes: 'make more': short for 'make more money' or 'increase profits'.
3.remarks: 'make more': “赚更多钱”或“增加利润”的简写。

4.original: He had mapped out a new advertising campaign he had been thinking about.
4.b1: He had also planned a new advertising campaign he had been thinking about.
4.cn: 他还规划了一个他一直在思考的全新广告活动。
4.ai_notes:
'mapped out': planned something in detail, often using a visual or logical structure.
'advertising campaign': a series of advertisements created to promote a product or service.
4.remarks:
'mapped out': 详细规划某事，通常使用视觉或逻辑结构。
'advertising campaign': 为推广产品或服务而创建的一系列广告。

5.original: When I left him, his parting remark was, I sure appreciate the chance to tell someone about some of my ideas.
5.b1: When I left, he said, "I really appreciate the chance to share my ideas."
5.cn: 当我离开时，他的临别赠言是：“我真的很感激有机会能向别人讲述我的一些想法。”
5.ai_notes:
'parting remark': the last thing someone says before they leave.
'I sure appreciate': a common, slightly informal way to say 'I really appreciate'.
5.remarks:
'parting remark': 某人离开前说的最后一句话。
'I sure appreciate': “我真的很感激”的一种常用且略显随意的说法。

6.original: We've got a good outfit, but I believe we can make it better.
6.b1: We have a good company, but I believe we can make it even better.
6.cn: 我们有一家不错的公司，但我相信我们可以让它变得更好。
6.ai_notes: 'outfit': informal slang for a company, organization, or group of people working together.
6.remarks: 'outfit': 非正式俚语，指公司、组织或一起工作的一群人。

7.original: My recommendation, of course, was for the third man.
7.b1: Of course, I recommended the third man.
7.cn: 当然，我的推荐人选是第三位男士。
7.ai_notes: 'recommendation': a suggestion that someone or something is good or suitable for a particular purpose.
7.remarks: 'recommendation': 关于某人或某物适合特定目的的建议。

8.original: It was a recommendation that coincided perfectly with the feelings of the company president.
8.b1: My recommendation matched exactly what the company president was thinking.
8.cn: 我的推荐与公司总裁的想法完全一致。
8.ai_notes: 'coincided': happened at the same time or matched perfectly with something else.
8.remarks: 'coincided': 同时发生或与某事完美吻合。

9.original: Believe in expansion, efficiency, new products, new processes, better schools, increased prosperity, Believe in and push for progress,
9.b1: Believe in growth, efficiency, new products, and progress, and always push for them.
9.cn: 相信扩张、效率、新产品、新流程、更好的学校和日益繁荣，相信并推动进步，
9.ai_notes:
'prosperity': the state of being successful and having a lot of money.
'push for': to work hard to achieve or promote something.
9.remarks:
'prosperity': 成功且拥有大量金钱的状态。
'push for': 为实现或促进某事而努力。

10.b1: Then you will be a leader.
10.cn: 这样你就会成为一名领导者。
10.ai_notes: 'leader': someone who guides others and influences their actions.
10.remarks: 'leader': 指导他人并影响其行为的人。

11.original: As a youngster, I had an opportunity to see how the different thinking of two leaders can make an amazing difference in the performance of followers.
11.b1: When I was young, I saw how different leaders can change how people perform.
11.cn: 小时候，我有机会看到两位领导者不同的思维方式如何对下属的表现产生惊人的影响。
11.ai_notes:
'youngster': a child or a young person.
'make an amazing difference': to have a very large and positive effect on a situation.
11.remarks:
'youngster': 儿童或年轻人。
'make an amazing difference': 对某种情况产生非常大且积极的影响。

12.original: I attended a country elementary school.
12.b1: I went to a small country elementary school.
12.cn: 我读的是一所乡村小学。
12.ai_notes: 'country elementary school': a primary school located in a rural or farming area.
12.remarks: 'country elementary school': 位于农村或农业地区的小学。

13.original: Eight grades, one teacher, and 40 children all jammed together inside four brick walls.
13.b1: There were eight grades, one teacher, and 40 children in one room.
13.cn: 八个年级，一位老师，40个孩子，全都挤在四面砖墙里。
13.ai_notes: 'jammed together': packed tightly into a small space.
13.remarks: 'jammed together': 紧紧地挤在一个狭小的空间里。

14.original: A new teacher was always a big deal.
14.b1: A new teacher was always a big event.
14.cn: 来一位新老师总是一件大事。
14.ai_notes: 'a big deal': an important event or something that attracts a lot of attention.
14.remarks: 'a big deal': 重要事件或引起广泛关注的事物。

15.original: Led by the big boys, the seventh and eighth graders, the pupils set out to see how much they could get away with.
15.b1: The older students led the others to see how much trouble they could cause.
15.cn: 在七、八年级大孩子的带领下，学生们开始试探他们能做出多少出格的事。
15.ai_notes:
'set out': started a plan or journey with a specific goal.
'get away with': to do something wrong without being punished for it.
15.remarks:
'set out': 以特定目标开始计划或旅程。
'get away with': 做错事而不受惩罚。

16.original: One year there was little more than chaos.
16.b1: One year, the school was almost in total chaos.
16.cn: 有一年，学校里几乎是一片混乱。
16.ai_notes: 'chaos': a state of total confusion and lack of order.
16.remarks: 'chaos': 完全混乱且缺乏秩序的状态。

17.original: Every day there were dozens of the usual school pranks, wars of spitballs and paper airplanes.
17.b1: Every day there were many pranks, like throwing spitballs and paper airplanes.
17.cn: 每天都有几十个常见的恶作剧，比如打纸弹和扔纸飞机。
17.ai_notes:
'pranks': practical jokes or mischievous acts done to tease others.
'spitballs': small balls of wet paper that children throw at each other.
17.remarks:
'pranks': 为了戏弄他人而做的恶作剧。
'spitballs': 孩子们互相投掷的小湿纸团。

18.original: Then there were the major incidents, such as locking the teacher outside the school for half a day at a time,
18.b1: There were also serious problems, like locking the teacher out of the school for half a day.
18.cn: 还有一些严重的事件，比如把老师锁在学校外面，一锁就是半天，
18.ai_notes: 'major incidents': serious or significant events that cause trouble.
18.remarks: 'major incidents': 引起麻烦的严重或重大事件。

19.original: or on another occasion the opposite, barricading her within the building for hours.
19.b1: Or the opposite, keeping her trapped inside the building for hours.
19.cn: 或者相反，把她关在教学楼里好几个小时。
19.ai_notes: 'barricading': blocking a door or entrance to prevent someone from entering or leaving.
19.remarks: 'barricading': 封锁门或入口以阻止他人进出。

20.original: Another day, each boy in the upper grades brought his dog into the schoolroom.
20.b1: Another day, every boy in the older grades brought his dog to class.
20.cn: 还有一天，高年级的每个男生都把自己的狗带进了教室。
20.ai_notes: 'upper grades': the classes for the oldest students in the school.
20.remarks: 'upper grades': 学校中高年级学生的班级。

21.original: Let me add that these children were not delinquents.
21.b1: I should add that these children were not bad kids.
21.cn: 我得补充一点，这些孩子并不是品行不良。
21.ai_notes: 'delinquents': young people who regularly commit crimes or behave in a way that breaks social rules.
21.remarks: 'delinquents': 经常犯罪或行为违反社会规则的年轻人。

22.original: Stealing, physical violence, and deliberate harm were not their objectives.
22.b1: They did not want to steal, hurt others, or cause real damage.
22.cn: 偷窃、身体暴力和故意伤害并不是他们的目的。
22.ai_notes: 'objectives': goals or the things someone is trying to achieve.
22.remarks: 'objectives': 目标或某人试图实现的事物。

23.original: They were healthy kids, conditioned by vigorous rural living, and needing an outlet for their tremendous pent-up energies and ingenuities.
23.b1: They were healthy, energetic kids who needed a way to use their extra energy and creativity.
23.cn: 他们是健康的孩子，在充满活力的乡村生活中长大，需要一个出口来释放他们巨大的精力和创造力。
23.ai_notes:
'conditioned': influenced or shaped by a specific environment.
'vigorous': full of energy and physical strength.
'pent-up': feelings or energy that are held inside and not expressed.
'ingenuities': clever or creative ways of solving problems.
23.remarks:
'conditioned': 受特定环境影响或塑造。
'vigorous': 充满活力和体力。
'pent-up': 被压抑在内心而未表达的情感或能量。
'ingenuities': 解决问题的巧妙或创造性的方法。

24.original: Well, the teacher somehow managed to stay with the school until the end of that year.
24.b1: The teacher managed to stay until the end of the year.
24.cn: 那位老师总算设法坚持到了那学年结束。
24.ai_notes: 'managed to': succeeded in doing something difficult.
24.remarks: 'managed to': 成功完成了某件困难的事。

25.original: To no one's surprise, there was a new teacher the following September.
25.b1: It was no surprise that there was a new teacher the next September.
25.cn: 不出所料，第二年九月换了一位新老师。
25.ai_notes: 'To no one's surprise': a common phrase used when something happens exactly as people expected.
25.remarks: 'To no one's surprise': 当事情完全如人们所料发生时使用的常用语。

26.original: The new teacher extracted strikingly different performance from the children.
26.b1: The new teacher got much better results from the children.
26.cn: 这位新老师让孩子们表现出了截然不同的状态。
26.ai_notes: 'extracted': in this context, it means to get or obtain a specific type of behavior from others.
26.remarks: 'extracted': 在此语境下，指从他人那里获得或引出某种行为。

27.original: She appealed to their personal pride and sense of respect.
27.b1: She appealed to their pride and their sense of respect.
27.cn: 她激发了他们的个人自尊心和尊重感。
27.ai_notes: 'appealed to': tried to influence someone by using their personal values or emotions.
27.remarks: 'appealed to': 试图通过利用某人的个人价值观或情感来影响他们。

28.original: She encouraged them to develop judgment.
28.b1: She encouraged them to think for themselves.
28.cn: 她鼓励他们培养判断力。
28.ai_notes: 'judgment': the ability to make sensible decisions based on careful thought.
28.remarks: 'judgment': 基于仔细思考做出明智决定的能力。

29.original: Each child was assigned a specific responsibility, like washing blackboards or cleaning erasers,
29.b1: Each child had a job, like cleaning the blackboard or the erasers.
29.cn: 每个孩子都被分配了具体的职责，比如擦黑板或清理板擦，
29.ai_notes: 'assigned': given a specific task or duty to perform.
29.remarks: 'assigned': 被分配了特定的任务或职责。

30.original: or practicing paper grading for the younger grades.
30.b1: Or helping to grade papers for the younger students.
30.cn: 或者练习为低年级学生批改作业。
30.ai_notes: 'paper grading': the act of checking and marking school assignments or tests.
30.remarks: 'paper grading': 检查和批改学校作业或考试的行为。

31.original: The new teacher found creative ways to use the energy that had been so misdirected a few months before.
31.b1: The new teacher found creative ways to use the energy that was wasted before.
31.cn: 新老师找到了创造性的方法，来利用几个月前被误导的那些精力。
31.ai_notes: 'misdirected': used in the wrong way or for the wrong purpose.
31.remarks: 'misdirected': 以错误的方式或为了错误的目的使用。

32.original: Her educational program was centered on building character.
32.b1: Her program focused on building good character.
32.cn: 她的教育计划是以培养品格为中心的。
32.ai_notes: 'centered on': focused primarily on one main subject or goal.
32.remarks: 'centered on': 主要集中于一个核心主题或目标。

33.original: Why did the children act like young devils one year and like young angels the next?
33.b1: Why did the children act so badly one year and so well the next?
33.cn: 为什么孩子们一年像小恶魔，下一年又像小天使？
33.ai_notes:
'young devils': a way to describe children who are behaving very badly.
'young angels': a way to describe children who are behaving very well.
33.remarks:
'young devils': 形容表现非常糟糕的孩子。
'young angels': 形容表现非常好的孩子。

34.original: The difference was the leader, their teacher.
34.b1: The difference was the leader, their teacher.
34.cn: 区别在于领导者，也就是他们的老师。
34.ai_notes: 'The difference was': a way to highlight the main reason for a change in results.
34.remarks: 'The difference was': 用于强调结果改变的主要原因。

35.original: In all honesty, we cannot blame the kids for playing pranks an entire school year.
35.b1: Honestly, we cannot blame the kids for playing pranks all year.
35.cn: 老实说，我们不能责怪孩子们整整一年都在搞恶作剧。
35.ai_notes: 'In all honesty': a phrase used to introduce a sincere or truthful opinion.
35.remarks: 'In all honesty': 用于引出真诚或真实意见的短语。

36.original: In each instance, the teacher set the pace.
36.b1: In every case, the teacher set the example.
36.cn: 在每一个例子中，都是老师定下了基调。
36.ai_notes: 'set the pace': to determine how fast or in what way things happen; to establish the standard of behavior.
36.remarks: 'set the pace': 决定事情发生的快慢或方式；建立行为标准。

37.original: The first teacher, deep down, didn't care whether the children made progress.
37.b1: Deep down, the first teacher did not care if the children learned anything.
37.cn: 那位老师内心深处并不在乎孩子们是否取得了进步。
37.ai_notes: 'deep down': in one's true feelings, even if they do not show it on the outside.
37.remarks: 'deep down': 在内心深处，即使外表没有表现出来。

38.original: She set no goals for the children.
38.b1: She did not set any goals for them.
38.cn: 她没有为孩子们设定任何目标。
38.ai_notes: 'set no goals': failed to create targets or plans for the students to achieve.
38.remarks: 'set no goals': 未能为学生设定目标或计划。

39.b1: She did not encourage them at all.
39.cn: 她一点也没有鼓励他们。
39.ai_notes: 'encourage': to give support, confidence, or hope to someone.
39.remarks: 'encourage': 给某人支持、信心或希望。

40.b1: She could not control her own anger.
40.cn: 她无法控制自己的脾气。
40.ai_notes: 'control her temper': to stay calm and not get angry easily.
40.remarks: 'control her temper': 保持冷静，不容易发火。
